title: Goals, Context, Outputs, Boundaries, and Done Conditions · Failure Case: Output Contract
summary: Write a production-ready prompt for a high-impact code and document change. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt output contract increment in a reusable goal-context-output-boundary template. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Specify output structure, granularity, evidence, and machine-readable constraints when they matter.
Provide context that can change the result and route the agent to authoritative sources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Output Contract
steps.instruction:

steps.title: Build Output Contract evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-output-contract-1hi2lc" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Output Contract into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-output-contract-1hi2lc
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Output Contract model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Output Contract applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Specify output structure, granularity, evidence, and machine-readable constraints when they matter.
